front: What are matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs) and their role in disease?
back: MMPs are zinc-dependent endopeptidases that degrade extracellular matrix. 23 human MMPs: collagenases (MMP-1,13), gelatinases (MMP-2,9), stromelysins (MMP-3,10). Roles: wound healing, cancer metastasis, arthritis, cardiovascular remodeling. Inhibitors (MMPIs) failed in trials due to side effects.
